meta.name: Operation NORTHSTORM
meta.description: 4-hour AI-assisted CTF — infiltrate BOREAS SYSTEMS and seize PROJECT LEVIATHAN

challenges.name: Company Info
challenges.description: Identify the Boreas Systems registration number. Look at the company website carefully.

challenges.name: Employee Directory
challenges.description: Find the organizational chart. Who reports to whom? The flag is embedded in the document.

challenges.name: Tech Stack Revealed
challenges.description: The careers page reveals what technologies Boreas uses. Look at everything on the page — not just what's visible.

challenges.name: Client Contracts
challenges.description: Old versions of websites sometimes reveal more than the current one. Find the client list.

challenges.name: DNS Reconnaissance
challenges.description: Enumerate the DNS records for boreas-systems.ctf. Some DNS servers allow zone transfers...

challenges.name: Follow the Money
challenges.description: Boreas published financial reports. One supplier received an unusually large payment. Submit the supplier name and amount in format: SUPPLIERNAME-AMOUNT (e.g., ACME-5000000)

hints.content: Look for a pattern in the filenames in /internal/

challenges.name: Configuration Leak
challenges.description: Web applications sometimes expose their configuration files. Check common paths.

challenges.name: Project Hints
challenges.description: Someone at Boreas sent a status report about 'the project.' Access their email and find the attachment.

challenges.name: Terminated Engineer
challenges.description: James Chen was terminated. His personnel file is on the corporate file share. What's the case reference number?

challenges.name: Password Reuse
challenges.description: Many employees don't change their default passwords. Try logging into the webmail with common credentials.

challenges.name: Mundane File Share
challenges.description: The corporate file share has public documents. Even mundane files can hide information — check the metadata.

challenges.name: The Project
challenges.description: The internal wiki has a page about 'Project Coordination.' Read it carefully — all of it.

challenges.name: Procurement Trail
challenges.description: PO-2847 is for something unusual. Follow the reference in the special instructions to find the detailed specifications.

challenges.name: Hidden Group
challenges.description: Active Directory groups can be nested. Some groups are hidden inside others. Enumerate recursively.

hints.content: Look at e.vasik's transitive group memberships

challenges.name: Lateral Movement
challenges.description: Credentials found in one system can unlock access to another. The IT admin's email has credentials for a different service.

challenges.name: Unreliable Guard
challenges.description: Badge access logs tell a story. Cross-reference the guard rotation schedule with the actual badge swipes. Someone is going where they shouldn't.

challenges.name: Domain Admin
challenges.description: The backup service account has more privileges than it should. Kerberoast it, crack the password, and use its misconfigured permissions to access the domain admin's secrets.

hints.content: svc-backup has Replicating Directory Changes rights (DCSync)

challenges.name: SCADA Discovery
challenges.description: The generator control system is on an isolated network. You'll need to pivot through a compromised Front Office host to reach it. Look for network documentation about VLAN 40.

challenges.name: Lights Out
challenges.description: Override the generator's safety systems and cause a thermal runaway. The interlock PLC must be bypassed via raw Modbus commands before the HMI controls will work. This is the collective gate — first team to complete it opens the Bunker for everyone.

hints.content: The interlock is on Modbus port 502. Check the System Architecture page on the HMI.

challenges.name: Default Credentials
challenges.description: The engineering workstation has a CI service account with default credentials. Check common service account names.

challenges.name: Research Data
challenges.description: The research database has structural specifications. Connect with discovered credentials and query the data.

challenges.name: Shipping Manifest
challenges.description: A delivery is arriving soon. The specifications document is in the build artifacts directory.

challenges.name: Simulation Archives
challenges.description: 47 simulation archives, but only a few contain bipedal references. Find the right .dat file and look inside the binary.

hints.content: grep the .log files for 'bipedal' first, then examine the corresponding .dat with strings

challenges.name: Source Code Secrets
challenges.description: Developers sometimes commit secrets to git and then remove them in a later commit. Check the repository history.

challenges.name: MIDNIGHT Test Series
challenges.description: The MIDNIGHT simulations were run after hours. The results are in a restricted directory. Find a way to access them.

challenges.name: Engineering Notes
challenges.description: The mechanical engineer's design files include a center-of-gravity analysis. But one worksheet is hidden. Unhide it.

challenges.name: Weapons Compartment
challenges.description: The research database has compartmentalized access. The weapons data is in a restricted schema. Find a way in.

hints.content: There's a search function in the public schema that runs with elevated privileges

challenges.name: Assembly Status
challenges.description: The manufacturing data shows what's been built and what's still pending. The final assembly record has nested metadata. Dig deep.

challenges.name: Deleted Schematic
challenges.description: The assembly repository had a schematic that was deleted 'per security policy.' But git never truly forgets. Recover the file and examine the raw source.

challenges.name: Simulation Video
challenges.description: A full integration simulation video was encrypted and 'deleted.' The private key is in the weapons database. The passphrase is in the source code. Chain them together.

hints.content: GPG private key is base64-encoded in compartment_b. Passphrase is LEGACY_PASSPHRASE in crypto_config.py.

challenges.name: OT Enumeration
challenges.description: The Bunker has three industrial controllers. Query each one's device identification (Modbus function code 43) and concatenate the three model numbers in network order.

challenges.name: Tail Controller
challenges.description: The tail controller has a diagnostic mode. Find the hint in the Lab's deployment playbooks, then use it to unlock the controller's hidden registers.

challenges.name: Leg Controller
challenges.description: The leg controller requires a specific calibration walk cycle to unlock. The sequence is documented in an Ansible playbook. Execute it via Modbus within the time window.

challenges.name: Arms Controller
challenges.description: The arms controller uses a rolling challenge-response. You need a number from the Front Office procurement records to compute the correct response.

challenges.name: Brain Access
challenges.description: The central controller speaks a custom binary protocol. Find the protocol documentation in the source code, gather the authentication credentials, and connect.

challenges.name: Combat System Seized
challenges.description: The override code is split across three zones. Assemble it from the website, the simulation data, and the assembly database. Enter it into the brain controller.

hints.content: Format: XXXX-XXXX-XXXX. Pieces from: A0 registration number, A6 MIDNIGHT-7 simulation ID, A8 assembly log integration code.
